message:
feat: multi-agent collaborative development updates

Consolidate ongoing multi-agent development work:

**Core:**
- keybinding.rs: Keybinding system refinements

**Demo Showcase:**
- app.rs, chrome.rs: Application updates
- action_timeline.rs, markdown_rich_text.rs: Screen refinements
- mod.rs, theme.rs: Module and theming updates
- screen_snapshots.rs: Test updates

**Extras:**
- text_effects.rs, visual_fx.rs: Effect refinements

**PTY:**
- lib.rs: Library updates
- input_forwarding.rs (new): Input forwarding support

**Render:**
- diff.rs, presenter.rs: Rendering optimizations

**Text:**
- editor.rs: Text editor updates

**Widgets:**
- command_palette: Palette refinements
- focus/spatial.rs: Spatial navigation updates
- measurable.rs: Measurement updates

These changes represent continuous collaborative development
across multiple agents working on the FrankenTUI codebase.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2vne.4","title":"Tree view overlay for harness (Esc Esc)","description":"Implement a minimal tree view panel or overlay using existing widgets; toggled via Esc Esc and designed for inline mode.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Tree view overlay for harness (Esc Esc)
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:13:47.767789745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T23:11:12.415192898Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["harness","ux","widgets"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vne.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vne","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:13:47.767789745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vne.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2vne.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:16:25.076303434Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":104,"issue_id":"bd-2vne.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Intent
- Provide a practical “tree” view similar to Pi’s UX cue. The tree can be a UI hierarchy, model state nodes, or a log category tree.
- Keep it minimal and demonstrative rather than a full inspector.

Implementation Notes
- Reuse `ftui-widgets::tree` or build a simple list-based tree if needed.
- Ensure overlay respects inline mode (UI region stays fixed, logs scroll above).

Acceptance Criteria
- Esc Esc toggles the tree view without flicker.
- Tree view does not interfere with input focus (Esc closes first).
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:13:54Z"}]}

Documentation Targets

- Docs accurately reflect default bindings and their rationale.

 {"id":"bd-2vne.6","title":"Keybinding action mapping + state machine helper","description":"Implement a reusable action-mapping layer that interprets KeyEvent stream into high-level actions based on input/task state and policy spec.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Keybinding action mapping + state machine helper
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:18:38.697889896Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T04:03:45.887230013Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T04:03:45.887134373Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["input","keybindings","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2vne.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vne","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:18:38.697889896Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vne.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vne.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:19:16.368220166Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2vne.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2vne.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:19:16.513723005Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":134,"issue_id":"bd-2vne.6","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Purpose
- Separate “what key was pressed” from “what action should the app take,” using the policy spec.
- Keep the logic reusable so harness and future apps can share it.

Behavioral Scope
- Inputs: KeyEvent stream + state flags (input_nonempty, task_running, modal_open, view_overlay).
- Output: High-level actions like ClearInput, CancelTask, ToggleTree, Quit, PassThrough.
- Includes explicit priority order (e.g., modal cancel before task cancel).

Acceptance Criteria
- Action mapping matches policy spec and is unit-tested.
- Minimal API surface, no tight coupling to harness UI structures.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:18:44Z"}]}

","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:19:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2vr","title":"Create comprehensive E2E test suite","description":"# Create comprehensive E2E test suite

## Background
End-to-end tests verify the complete rendering pipeline from widget construction through terminal output. Must test real terminal interactions, not mocked abstractions.

## Technical Requirements

### E2E Test Infrastructure
- `scripts/e2e_test.sh` master script
- Individual test scripts per feature area
- JSON output for CI parsing
- Verbose mode with full trace output
- Exit codes: 0=pass, 1=fail, 2=skip

### Test Categories

#### 1. Rendering Tests (scripts/e2e/render/)
- `hello_world.sh` - Basic text output
- `colors.sh` - Full color palette rendering
- `unicode.sh` - Emoji, CJK, RTL text
- `styles.sh` - Bold, italic, underline, strikethrough
- `hyperlinks.sh` - OSC 8 hyperlink rendering

#### 2. Layout Tests (scripts/e2e/layout/)
- `flex_horizontal.sh` - Row layout constraints
- `flex_vertical.sh` - Column layout constraints
- `grid.sh` - 2D grid positioning
- `nested.sh` - Complex nested layouts
- `resize.sh` - Terminal resize handling

#### 3. Widget Tests (scripts/e2e/widgets/)
- `block.sh` - Borders, titles, padding
- `paragraph.sh` - Text wrapping, alignment
- `table.sh` - Column sizing, selection
- `list.sh` - Selection, scrolling
- `input.sh` - Text entry, cursor

#### 4. Input Tests (scripts/e2e/input/)
- `keyboard.sh` - Key events, modifiers
- `mouse.sh` - Click, scroll, drag
- `paste.sh` - Bracketed paste mode
- `focus.sh` - Focus in/out events

#### 5. Integration Tests (scripts/e2e/integration/)
- `full_app.sh` - Complete application lifecycle
- `inline_mode.sh` - Scrollback-native rendering
- `alt_screen.sh` - Alternate screen buffer
- `raw_mode.sh` - Raw terminal mode transitions

### Test Script Template
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/../common.sh"

TEST_NAME="render/hello_world"
log_start "${TEST_NAME}"

# Run the test binary
output=$(cargo run --example hello_world 2>&1) || fail "Example failed to run"

# Verify output
assert_contains "${output}" "Hello"
assert_no_errors "${output}"

log_pass "${TEST_NAME}"
```

### Logging Requirements
All test scripts must provide detailed logging:

**Start Phase:**
- Timestamp (ISO 8601)
- Test name and category
- Environment info (TERM, COLORTERM, shell)
- Configuration parameters

**Progress Phase:**
- Major checkpoint announcements
- Timing for each phase
- Resource usage snapshots

**Failure Phase:**
- Full context dump
- Expected vs actual comparison
- Stack trace if available
- Terminal state capture
- Reproduction command

**Success Phase:**
- Duration breakdown
- Resource usage summary
- Bytes emitted count

### common.sh Functions
```bash
log_start() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] START: $1"; }
log_progress() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] PROGRESS: $1"; }
log_pass() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] PASS: $1 (${SECONDS}s)"; }
log_fail() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] FAIL: $1 - $2" >&2; exit 1; }
fail() { log_fail "${TEST_NAME}" "$1"; }
assert_contains() { [[ "$1" == *"$2"* ]] || fail "Expected to contain: $2"; }
assert_no_errors() { [[ "$1" != *"error"* ]] || fail "Found error in output"; }
capture_terminal_state() { echo "TERM=$TERM COLORTERM=$COLORTERM"; }
```

### CI Integration
- JUnit XML output for test runners
- Parallel execution where safe
- Retry logic for flaky tests (max 2 retries)
- Artifact collection on failure (logs, screenshots, terminal state)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Master `e2e_test.sh` script with `--verbose` and `--json` flags
- [ ] `common.sh` with all helper functions
- [ ] 5+ rendering tests with detailed logging
- [ ] 5+ layout tests with detailed logging
- [ ] 5+ widget tests with detailed logging
- [ ] 5+ input tests with detailed logging
- [ ] 3+ integration tests with detailed logging
- [ ] JSON output mode for CI parsing
- [ ] Verbose trace mode showing all operations
- [ ] CI-friendly exit codes (0/1/2)
- [ ] Test duration < 60s total
- [ ] JUnit XML report generation

 {"id":"bd-2w5","title":"Implement view_string() easy mode adapter","description":"# Implement view_string() Easy Mode Adapter

## Background
Plan Section 0.8.2 and 12.3 describe a "Easy mode adapter" for trivial apps and prototyping. This allows developers to return a String from view() while still going through the full kernel pipeline.

## Why This Matters
- Enables quick prototyping without learning full Frame API
- Familiar model for developers coming from Bubbletea
- Keeps kernel discipline (String still goes through Frame)
- Lower barrier to entry for simple apps

## Technical Design

### Model Trait Extension
```rust
/// Optional trait for models that want string-based views
pub trait StringModel: Model {
    /// Return view as string (styled text with markup)
    fn view_string(&self) -> String;
}

/// Blanket implementation: view_string -> Frame
impl<M: StringModel> Model for M {
    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        let text = self.view_string();
        let parsed = Text::from_markup(&text);
        frame.render_widget(parsed, frame.area());
    }
}
```

### Alternative: Wrapper Type
```rust
/// Wrapper that converts StringModel to Model
pub struct StringModelAdapter<M: StringModel> {
    inner: M,
}

impl<M: StringModel> Model for StringModelAdapter<M> {
    type Message = M::Message;
    
    fn update(&mut self, msg: Self::Message) -> Cmd<Self::Message> {
        self.inner.update(msg)
    }
    
    fn view(&self, frame: &mut Frame) {
        let text = self.inner.view_string();
        let parsed = Text::from_markup(&text);
        frame.render_widget(Paragraph::new(parsed), frame.area());
    }
}
```

### Pipeline Preservation
String -> Text/Segments -> Frame draw -> Diff -> Presenter

This ensures:
- Markup is properly parsed
- Styles are correctly applied
- Width measurement is correct
- Diffing works normally
- All kernel guarantees hold

## Usage Example
```rust
struct Counter { count: i32 }

impl StringModel for Counter {
    fn view_string(&self) -> String {
        format\!("[bold]Count:[/] {}", self.count)
    }
}

fn main() -> Result<()> {
    App::string_model(Counter { count: 0 })
        .screen_mode(ScreenMode::Inline { ui_height: 1 })
        .run()
}
```

## API Options

### Option 1: StringModel trait
```rust
App::string_model(model)
```

### Option 2: view() callback
```rust
App::new(model)
    .view(|state| format\!("Count: {}", state.count))
```

### Option 3: Both
Support both patterns for flexibility.

## Tests Required
- [ ] view_string() produces correct Frame output
- [ ] Markup parsing works correctly
- [ ] Multi-line strings render properly
- [ ] Width measurement correct
- [ ] Style application correct
- [ ] Diffing works as expected

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] StringModel trait or equivalent
- [ ] Integration with App/Program
- [ ] Proper pipeline (not bypass)
- [ ] Documentation with examples
- [ ] Tests for all paths
